page: Amazon Web Services c7g.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the custom AWS Graviton3 processor. Built on the arm64 architecture with ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 cores, this server features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. No GPU accelerators are present in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates that the c7g.2xlarge delivers average performance across most standard compute and memory tasks. In stress-ng, it achieves a single-core score of 1532.98 and a multi-core score of 12238.41. Memory bandwidth tests show 774.89 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 146.42 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, the instance exhibits weaknesses in specific workloads, such as bzip3 decompression, where it scores in the bottom 25% with 7.55 GB/sec for single-threaded and 35.95 GB/sec for multi-threaded operations. Passmark CPU encryption and overall CPU scores also fall into the weak category.

The instance demonstrates average capabilities for database operations, with a Redis SET performance of 13.27 million operations per second and a static web serving throughput of 15.45 GB/sec. For machine learning, it processes prompts on a small 135M LLM model at 395.61 tokens per second, but struggles with medium 7B models, dropping to a poor rating of 14.13 tokens per second for prompt processing. Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and lack of local storage, this instance is suited for compute-bound workloads such as software compilation, web serving, and distributed analytics that do not require high local disk I/O or massive memory footprints.
description: Amazon Web Services c7g.2xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton3 processor based on the ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 architecture. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB of memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 8116.0 and a static web serving throughput of 15.45 GB/sec. However, it shows performance trade-offs, scoring in the bottom 25% for bzip3 decompression and Passmark CPU encryption. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the c7g.2xlarge is designed for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, web serving, and lightweight database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.2xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 8 Graviton3 cores, 16 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average performance for web serving and software compilation.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.2xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 Graviton3 cores, 16 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton3 processors and a balanced memory-to-core ratio for distributed, compute-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 physical cores on arm64 architecture
16.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average performance in software compilation and static web serving
Reduced efficiency in bzip3 decompression and cryptographic tasks
Suited for web serving, application servers, and software development
